title: China to Mark Zuckerberg's Meta: You cannot buy this company
content: China has blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, citing national security concerns and potential technology leakage. This move follows reports of Beijing restricting American capital in its AI sector. The decision stems from Manus's "general AI agent" technology, developed in China but moved to Singapore, raising fears of intellectual property transfer to US firms.

title: 'If anyone can challenge Virat, it's ... ': Kohli's record called 'stepping stone'
content: Virat Kohli became the first batter to cross 9,000 IPL runs, further cementing his legacy of consistency and dominance. Irfan Pathan said the milestone is “just a stepping stone” for Kohli. At Arun Jaitley Stadium, Kohli played an unbeaten, match-winning knock. With eight centuries, strong form, and Orange Cap contention, he remains central to RCB’s success this season and their strong playoff push.

title: Sun Pharma buys US-based Organon for $12 billion in biggest-ever overseas pharma deal
content: India's largest pharma company, Sun Pharma, has made its biggest overseas acquisition, buying Organon & Co for $11.8 billion. This deal significantly expands Sun Pharma's global presence and product range. It will double the company's revenue and EBITDA. The acquisition positions Sun Pharma as a major player in women's health and biosimilars. This strategic move strengthens Sun Pharma's position in the global pharmaceutical market.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 mn
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ misses Rs 1780 crore mark worldwide by whisker
content: Dhurandhar 2 experienced a major box office drop on its 40th day. The film earned Rs 1.05 crore in India and Rs 0.25 crore overseas. Its worldwide gross collection reached Rs 1779.02 crore. Competitors Bhooth Bangla and Michael also saw reduced earnings on Monday. The movie's performance reflects a decline after its initial surge.

title: 'MS Dhoni might play if CSK gets out of tournament': Ashwin drops truth bomb
content: Since the start of the tournament, Dhoni has been dealing with a calf injury. He was expected to return for CSK’s last match against Gujarat Titans, but that did not happen.

title: Apple's next CEO John Ternus tells employees what Apple won't do with AI
content: Days after being named Apple's next CEO, John Ternus walked into a town hall and told employees what Apple won't do with AI: ship technology for technology's sake. With Siri still broken after two years of missed promises, Ternus laid out a two-part AI philosophy—one focused on internal data, the other on products that actually work. The question is whether Apple's restrained approach wins or falls further behind.

title: Infosys slips out of top 10 valued companies in country
content: Bengaluru-headquartered Infosys has fallen out of India's top 10 most valued companies, now ranking 11th, while Tata Consultancy Services has dropped to sixth. These shifts have been attributed to artificial intelligence-driven disruptions impacting the business models of major Indian software exporters.  The information technology sector saw significant sell-offs on Monday.

title: NFL Trade Rumor: Cowboys send Pickens to Bills for Coleman, picks
content: The Dallas Cowboys face a crucial decision regarding wide receiver George Pickens. CBS Sports suggests a trade. ClutchPoints suggest Pickens could be acquired in exchange for draft capital and wide receiver Keon Coleman. However, recent reports indicate the Buffalo Bills are committed to WR Keon Coleman. This alters the proposed trade scenario and leaving Pickens’ future with Dallas uncertain. But he wants a trade. So, we are back to square one.

title: Fact check: Clavicular pregnancy rumour after 10-day romance debunked
content: Clavicular, whose real name is Braden Peters, went viral after claims he is expecting a child with a girlfriend of 10 days. The rumor came from a viral post and a misunderstood livestream. There is no proof of pregnancy. The story is false, while real concerns remain about his health, recent hospital stay, and ongoing controversies.

title: 'I cried harder' over painful parenting moment with daughter: Serena Williams
content: Serena Williams shared a real parenting moment where she had to discipline her daughter, even though it made them both cry. She stood by her decision after her daughter missed a sleepover for not following bedtime rules. The story shows that discipline is hard but important. Serena Williams continues to balance motherhood with honesty and strong values.

title: Jhalmuri politics, EVM fears & PM Modi's letter - Key points from WB phase II campaigning
content: West Bengal's Phase 2 assembly election campaign concluded with intense polarization, marked by high-decibel rallies and sharp political attacks. Prime Minister Modi's symbolic outreach and Mamata Banerjee's street-level campaigning defined competing narratives, while record Phase 1 turnout intensified voter mobilization efforts.

title: Great Nicobar Island: Why China is worried about India's new outpost
content: Global maritime trade faces disruptions with the Strait of Hormuz closure impacting energy supplies. India is bolstering its maritime capabilities, investing heavily in port infrastructure like the Great Nicobar Island Project. This strategic move aims to enhance economic and military influence, securing trade routes and reducing reliance on foreign transhipment hubs.

title: 'Don't cry later': UP cop on poll duty warns TMC candidate, video triggers big row
content: In a video going viral, Ajay Pal Sharma was seening warning Jehangir Khan's family: "Tell him, we’ll deal properly with him if his men continue to threaten...don’t cry or regret it then."​​​ Hailing Ajay Sharma's as "Singham" of Uttar Pradesh, BJP's I-T department chief Amit Malviya said the UP encounter specialist "wasted no time in setting the tone, firmly reading the riot act to the family members of Abhishek Banerjee’s close aide, Jehangir Khan". ​

title: IPL Purple Cap: Bhuvneshwar Kumar surges ahead after record-breaking spell
content: Bhuvneshwar Kumar's exceptional 3-wicket haul for 5 runs propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a dominant victory over Delhi Capitals. This stellar performance saw him claim the IPL 2026 Purple Cap with 14 wickets. The match was largely decided by a devastating powerplay spell from Bhuvneshwar and Hazlewood, which reduced DC to 13 for 6.

title: 'I am not a rapist': Trump erupts in interview as media dinner shooting fallout deepens
content: President Trump angrily denounced a journalist for reading excerpts from a suspect's manifesto during a 60 Minutes interview, forcefully rejecting accusations of sexual assault. The incident occurred after a security scare at a media dinner where a suspect armed with multiple weapons was intercepted by Secret Service agents.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for April 28
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for April 28, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: IPL 2026 Points Table: RCB close in on PBKS after thumping win over DC
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru continued their dominant IPL 2026 campaign, securing a commanding nine-wicket victory over Delhi Capitals. This win propelled RCB to 12 points, consolidating their second position and narrowing the gap with league leaders Punjab Kings. The emphatic performance also significantly boosted RCB's Net Run Rate, now the best in the tournament.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 40: Ranveer Singh film eyes 'Pushpa 2’s all-time domestic record
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sara Arjun, is on the verge of becoming the second highest-grossing Indian film of all time globally, with a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,777.52 crore, just Rs. 10.54 crore away from surpassing 'Baahubali 2: The Conclusion's Rs. 1,788.06 crore record. On Day 40, the film's India net stands at Rs. 1,130.69 crore, approximately Rs. 103 crore away from 'Pushpa 2: The Rule's all-time domestic record of Rs. 1,234.1 crore.

title: 0% tariffs, visas, $20 bn investment: How India benefits in trade deal with NZ
content: India and New Zealand have signed a 'once-in-a-generation' trade pact, granting Indian exporters duty-free access to New Zealand. The agreement aims to double bilateral trade to $5 billion in five years and includes a $20 billion investment commitment from New Zealand. Both nations are optimistic about the pact's potential to boost economic growth and cooperation across various sectors.

title: Hazlewood, Bhuvi wreak havoc as RCB blow away DC for 75 in 9-wicket win
content: Capitals were going at a rate of nearly a-run-a-wicket before they finished the Powerplay at 13/6. Call it the worst batting performance in the Powerplay or the best spell of fast bowling in a Powerplay, those six overs served as a staunch reminder of how cricket can be a great leveller.

title: Ashwin Kaushal: Rishi Kapoor idol, now plays Ranbir’s father
content: Ranbir Kapoor, Alia Bhatt and Vicky Kaushal-starrer Love & War has been steadily building buzz, and a new behind-the-scenes revelation has now added to the intrigue. Senior actor Ashwin Kaushal, who plays Ranbir Kapoor’s father in the film, has shared how the role came his way. Ashwin also spoke about his experience of working with Ranbir Kapoor, highlighting the actor’s humility despite his stardom.

title: From '12/10' summit to silent showdown: China's subtle economic play against US
content: Despite a positive meeting, China is strategically expanding economic leverage against the US. New regulations target companies shifting supply chains and restrict foreign tech in state data centers. Beijing is building pressure tools, moving beyond trade, as both nations navigate a complex geopolitical landscape and competition for critical resources.

title: Bhuvneshwar joins Bumrah in elite IPL club, becomes second pacer ever to ...
content: Bhuvneshwar Kumar achieved a significant IPL milestone, taking his 20th three-wicket haul to join Jasprit Bumrah as the only pacers with 20 or more such performances. This came during a devastating powerplay spell where he, along with Josh Hazlewood, ripped through the Delhi Capitals' batting lineup, leaving them stunned at 8 for 6.

title: ‘Basava Sri’ award for H.D. Deve Gowda
content: Mr. Gowda apologised for not being able to participate in the Basava Jayanti programme to receive the award owing to his health

title: M.P. Assembly passes resolution to implement women’s reservation after delimitation amid uproar by Opposition
content: The Opposition members walked out of the House after Speaker Narendra Singh Tomar declined to take up the bill, ruling that a government resolution on the issue — scheduled to be moved by Chief Minister Mohan Yadav — had already been listed

title: Haryana Assembly passes women empowerment resolution; Congress boycotts session
content: Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini said that the constitution of the women empowerment committee would strengthen the women in line with the vision of Prime Minister Narendra Modi

title: Citizens, NGOs step in to quench the thirst of urban animals
content: Animal welfare activists in the city have taken initiatives to ensure stray animals have access to water and are raising awareness about it as well

title: No rest for the wicked: Even as Tamil Nadu comes down from its election high, tensions are still rising
content: The issues vary from the advisability of Chief Minister M.K.Stalin taking a holiday in Kodaikkanal to the comparative performance of Tamil Nadu with other southern States with respect to the IT sector to the possibility of a coalition regime getting formed due to the presence of the Vijay-led TVK

title: Woman found dead in decomposed state
content: The incident came to light after neighbours noticed a strong foul smell emanating from her house and alerted the police

title: Bengaluru to host pan-India IIT summit on May 16
content: Themed around ‘Technology Sovereignty’, the one-day conclave is expected to bring together 2,000 participants from across 23 IITs, alongside policy makers, technology leaders, startup founders and investors

title: Residents urge BMRCL to include ‘Bilekahalli’ in IIMB metro station name on Bengaluru’s Pink Line metro
content: The residents have proposed that incorporating both the institutional and locality names would strike a balance between modern infrastructure and local heritage

title: Tamil Nadu does it again with double-digit economic growth
content: The State registers 10.83% real economic growth rate during 2025-26, according to the recently-released data of the Union Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation

title: 13 Indian fishermen from Nagapattinam attacked, robbed of their catch mid-sea; one suspected Sri Lankan fisherman nabbed
content: The Sri Lankan fisherman, identified as Ayanthan of  Kankesanthurai, was captured by some Nagapattinam fishermen. He was handed over to the Department of Fisheries

title: Kochi Corporation proposes facilitating nightlife along Marine Drive Walkway
content: Improved lighting and security, a premium cafe, night shops, and an open stage among the facilities proposed

title: Hot Westerly winds set to increase temperatures from May 1 in city
content: However, this rise in temperature in coastal regions will be brought down by the daily onset of the sea breeze that will bring some respite for the coastal areas, says a senior meteorologist

title: Amid fresh unrest, Home Secretary reviews Manipur security situation
content: The security vacuum created due to withdrawal of around 8,500 Central armed police forces for deployment during  West Bengal elections, and new areas of conflict between Kuki and Naga communities among the issues discussed

title: Proposal to increase retirement age of judicial officers in Tamil Nadu under consideration: Chief Justice
content: “Most probably, it will be done. It is only a matter of time,” the Chief Justice told senior counsel Vijay Narayan on Monday

title: Internal reservation: Nomadic and micro communities to question latest matrix in Karnataka HIgh Court
content: A meeting of the Federation of Untouchable Nomadic Communities of Karnataka has decided to continue its legal fight to secure a separate category in internal reservation for 59 communities

title: Ladakh groups seek meeting with Shah and steps to build trust
content: Home Minister to visit Union Territory from April 30; activist Wangchuk says govt. should withdraw cases filed against 83 persons held during last year’s protest for Statehood and inclusion in Sixth Schedule; L-G announces creation of five new districts

title: Punjab power outages: SAD blames AAP government’s failure
content: Power Minister Sanjeev Arora, meanwhile, asserted that Punjab remains firmly in control as electricity demand skyrocketed from 7,900 MW to over 12,000 MW within ten days

title: 12-storey-high bridge being built over Sabarmati for bullet train project
content: Foundation and substructure works of bridge over; steps taken to ensure smooth flow of water in the river during construction work

title: Cabinet reshuffle should take place and everyone should get opportunity, says Karnataka Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao
content: Karnataka Health Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao has said that there are several seniors and experienced leaders in the Congress and they should also get opportunity in the Cabinet reshuffle

title: Water supply disruption in Kerala’s Thiruvananthapuram on April 30
content: 72 mld and 75 mld water treatment plants at Aruvikkara will be shut down for the installation of a new transformer and other maintenance works

title: Punjab journalists complain of censorship by AAP Government
content: The Press Di Azaadi Bahal Karo Sangharsh Committee says copyright strikes and IT Rules takedown notices are stifling media voices in the State

title: Watch: Telangana census: your questions answered
content: Director of Census Operations in Telangana, Bharati Hollikeri, in an interview to The Hindu, speaks on the process, if any documents are asked for during the process, how can people crosscheck identity of enumerators during field visits and other aspects.

title: CAPFs go ‘all out’ to win ‘trust’, foster ‘peace’ ahead of last phase of Bengal polls
content: Over 100 armoured and anti-riot vehicles deployed across sensitive areas to instil confidence among voters, says official; security presence has “fostered trust and an atmosphere of peace”, says CRPF

title: Baby delivered onboard Murdeshwar-Bengaluru train at Bantwal Railway Station in Karnataka
content: She was found to be in distress and experiencing heavy bleeding when the train arrived at Bantwal railway station at 7.22 p.m.

title: ₹127.69 crore has been restored to victims of Rose Valley Group scam: ED
content: The ED has attached or seized movable and immovable properties with a book value of about ₹1,568 crore in West Bengal, Odisha, Assam, and Tripura

title: J&K court orders release of AAP MLA after 8 months of jail, says ‘PSA appears an unjustified exercise’
content: The court ordered the release of Mr. Malik from Kathua jail in Jammu; he was arrested on September 8, 2025 following his “inappropriate remarks” against then Deputy Commissioner Harvinder Singh

title: Madras High Court commutes death sentence of a man convicted of repeated sexual assault on minor daughter
content: The death penalty is final, immediate, and irreversible. It extinguishes not only life but also the possibility of repentance, remorse, or moral transformation, says the Madurai Bench

title: Minister refutes allegations of delay in caste certificates to Talwar community
content: Priyank Kharge was responding to allegations by the Talwar community regarding the delay in the issuance of Scheduled Tribes certificates

title: Verify your or your parents/grandparents details, get mapped with SIR 2002: Telangana CEO
content: Currently, matching or linking of the electors registered in the present roll with either their name or their relative’s name in the last SIR held in 2002 is being undertaken, the Chief Electoral Officer of Telangana said

title: Ties with India unaffected, says Nepal Envoy ahead of Foreign Secretary’s Kathmandu visit
content: Shankar Sharma said Nepal’s electricity exports to India will grow and added that the Motihari-Amlekhganj pipeline has helped his country deal with the energy crisis following the U.S.-Israel war on Iran

title: PM to flag off Banaras-Pune, Ayodhya-Mumbai express trains
content: The introduction of the two new weekly trains will link Uttar Pradesh with Maharashtra via Madhya Pradesh; the number of operational Amrit Bharat Express train services will rise to 64

title: HC asks govt. to come up with rehabilitation scheme for  transpersons at the taluk level
content: It asks the government to undo, as far as possible, the historical wrongs inflicted on transpersons, secure for them a life of dignity, equality, and meaningful inclusion as equal participants in the social order, and implement welfare measures for them to attain full development

title: Congress targets Centre for lack of consensus on joint statement at BRICS meeting
content: Officials say the lack of consensus had stemmed from sharp differences among member states on the evolving situation in West Asia

title: The play, Swatantryada Ota, wins the hearts of theatre-loving audience in Shivamogga
content: Swatantryada Ota, the play, has been produced by Shivamogga Rangayana with the financial support of the State government in its budget

title: Trump Slams Democrats Over Shutdown, Sets June 1 Deadline For Final Bill
content: US President Donald Trump on Tuesday lambasted the Democrats over the ongoing funding deadlock for the Department of Homeland Security (DHS).

title: King Charles Meets Trump In Bid To Salvage Ties
content: Britain's King Charles III met Donald Trump at the White House Monday, kicking off a high-stakes state visit shadowed by transatlantic tensions and a new alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

title: Probe Agency Disburses Rs 127.69 Crore To 1.73 Lakh Rose Valley Scam Victims
content: As of April 2026, the coordinated effort has successfully navigated multiple phases of fund recovery, returning life savings to over 1.7 lakh investors, according to a press release.

title: Two Shot Dead, One Injured In Firing Incident In Punjab
content: Two persons were shot dead and one sustained injuries after unidentified persons opened fire at them in Punjab's Batala late Monday night, police said.

title: Iraqi President Nominates Ali al-Zaidi As PM-Designate
content: Iraq's newly elected president nominated businessman Ali al-Zaidi as the country's prime minister-designate on Monday, after the country's leaders yielded to US pressure not to support the bid of...

title: Trump, Melania Slam Jimmy Kimmel For "Widow" Joke
content: US President Donald Trump called Monday for comedian Jimmy Kimmel to be fired for likening First Lady Melania Trump to an "expectant widow," in a joke made days before a third alleged attempt to assassinate Trump.

title: Biryani, Watermelon, Then 4 Deaths: A Tragedy That Played Out Over 12 Hours
content: Food poisoning is being suspected behind the suspicious deaths of the four family members within a gap of 12 hours.

title: Adani Total Gas' Q4 Profit Rises 9%, Revenue Jumps Nearly 17%
content: Adani Total Gas Limited (ATGL) on Monday reported a 9 per cent year-on-year (YoY) increase in its net profit for the quarter of FY26 ended March 31.

title: Trump Discusses Iran Offer To Open Straits Of Hormuz
content: US President Donald Trump on Monday met his national security team to discuss Iran's latest proposal to open the Hormuz Strait, the crucial waterway for transporting one-fifth of the global oil supplies.

title: 465-Foot Russian Superyacht Worth Over $500 Million Crosses Hormuz Strait
content: Nord left a Dubai marina at around 1400 GMT on Friday, crossed the strait on Saturday morning, and arrived in Muscat early on Sunday,

title: Trump Holds Talks With Security Team Over Iran Proposal To Reopen Hormuz
content: Speaking on the proposal sent by Iran, White House Spokesperson Karoline Leavitt told reporters that "the proposal was being discussed."

title: 'Left-Wing Cult Of Hatred': White House On Trump Dinner Shooting
content: Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said the incident, for which a suspect is due in court on Monday, was the third assassination attempt targeting Republican Trump in the past two years.

title: Trump Event Shooter Charged With 'Attempt To Assassinate' US President
content: Cole Tomas Allen: Accused Donald Trump event shooter charged with 'attempt to assassinate' US President

title: 'Iranians Very Skilful, Humiliating US': German Chancellor As Talks Stall
content: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said he did not see what exit strategy the US was pursuing in the Iran war

title: Man Digs Up Sister's Skeleton, Carries It To Bank For Rs 19,300 Balance
content: When Jitu approached the bank to withdraw the Rs 19,300 remaining balance in his sister's account, the bank manager refused, saying either the account holder must be present or he must furnish legal heir documents.

title: High Court Orders Release Of J&K AAP MLA Detained Under Public Safety Act
content: Mehraj Malik was arrested under this law in September last year after he had some heated argument with the district magistrate of Doda

title: 2 Cows Killed, 6 Injured After Chewing Crude Bombs In Tamil Nadu
content: Among the injured is a pregnant cow, with veterinary assessments indicating severe trauma to the animals' jaws.

title: Trinamool Worker Shot At By Unknown People, Hospitalised With 3 Bullets
content: The 30-year-old Trinamool Congress worker was hit by three bullets, following which residents rushed him to hospital

title: J&K bans education group Siraj-ul-Uloom over ‘Jamaat links’
content: the educational institution based in Shopian was sealed on monday . it has been declared "unlawful" under Unlawful Activities Act, 1967 . the move is based on a report of the shopian police chief .

title: Rajnath lands in Kyrgyzstan to attend SCO def ministers meet, may discuss West Asia crisis
content: this year’s SCO meeting is taking place against the backdrop of west Asia conflict . SCO, one of the largest political & economic organisations of the region, may discuss measures to reduce the impact of the conflict, according to the defence ministry .

title: Ahead of Amit Shah's visit, Ladakh expands from 2 to 7 districts
content: ladakh's number of districts rose from two to seven after five more were added . lieutenant governor says move fulfils a long-pending demand . the move would "strengthen grassroots governance" and improve service delivery in remote areas .

title: Vande Bharat derailment: Coach slips off tracks at Pune station
content: V ande Bharat derailment: Coach slips off tracks at Pune station (Source: X@ians_india) ​ The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. title: NIA court extends Shabir Shah’s custody in 1996 mob violence case till May 6
content: NIA court extends remand of separatist leader Shabir Ahmad Shah till may 6 . the accused was prima facie involved in serious offences punishable under the Unlawful Activities Prevention Act (UAPA) and Arms Act .

title: Mob lynches Varanasi businessman after his car hits woman
content: manish Singh was returning home from his factory when his car hit a woman . the 36-year-old businessman was lynched by a mob in the area of Varanasi . villagers and the woman's family surrounded him and started assaulting him with sticks and bricks .

title: Rajya Sabha chairman accepts merger of 7 AAP MPs with BJP
content: the governing party's strength in the 245-member upper House now stands at 113 . parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju said the chairman has accepted merger of the seven MPs with BJP . but aAP MP Sanjay Singh had written to Radhakrishnan seeking disqualification of the MPs .

title: Will be back soon for oath ceremony of Bengal’s 1st BJP govt, asserts Modi
content: Rohit Khanna covers sectors like government finance, economy, industry and issues related to financial crime.

title: Foreign secretary may hold 1st bilateral meet with Nepal's Balen Shah govt next month
content: foreign secretary is likely to travel to Nepal next month . the visit could take place on may 11-12 . both sides see the visit as an opportunity to further expand collaboration in multiple sectors . it was proposed when external affairs minister S Jaishankar met his counterpart .

title: Marathi issue: Maharashtra puts on hold action against auto, cab drivers
content: Somit Sen, Senior Editor at The Times of India, Mumbai. He covers stories on Power beat in Maharashtra and on Oil & Gas. He also covers RTO, BEST (Mumbai’s public transport buses), transport ministry, Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation, interstate transport (trucks/tempos) and the fleetcabs. Read More

title: Two teens speeding on bike crash into senior citizen at Mumbai’s Marine Drive; all dead
content: Mateen Hafeez, special correspondent at The Times of India in Mumbai, reports on terrorism, underworld, cybercrime and organized crime syndicates. He also writes about the jails in Maharashtra and focuses on human interest stories. He has covered the Ghatkopar bomb blast, Vile Parle bomb blast, Mulund train blast, train serial blasts in 2006, 26/11 terror attacks and Pune's German Bakery bomb blast. He has a special interest in Urdu fiction written by Ibn-e-Safi. Read More

title: India submits its new climate action pledges to UN body, flags conditions to fulfill promise
content: . Vishwa Mohan is Senior Editor at The Times of India. He writes on environment, climate change, agriculture, water resources and clean energy, tracking policy issues and climate diplomacy. He has been covering Parliament since 2003 to see how politics shaped up domestic policy and India’s position at global platform. Before switching over to explore sustainable development issues, Vishwa had covered internal security and investigative agencies for more than a decade. Read More

title: Bengal polls: Even after SIR, 23 of 142 seats have more woman voters in 2nd phase
content: women voters outnumbered men in 23 out of the 142 Assembly segments going to polls on Wednesday . topping the list is Jadavpur, which now has 11,759 more women voters than men . panihati, where the BJP has fielded the RG Kar victim's mother as its candidate .

title: Bengal polls: Shah urges people to vote fearlessly, says forces will stay back for 60 days
content: amit Shah asks people to vote fearlessly without worrying about the "goons" of CM Mamata Banerjee . central armed police forces (CAPFs) have been deployed everywhere . he says a govt will come to office after elections .

title: Death bridges LoC divide as relatives in PoK bid farewell across river
content: a coffin set by the narrow Kishanganga river in Keran sector of J&K became a bridge of sight, if not touch . a funeral turned into a cross-border moment of shared mourning . the coffin was brought to the river’s edge, allowing tycoons to reach the river .
